message:
feat(dcg): expand DCG status and tooling

Expand DCG (Destructive Command Guard) functionality:
- root.go: adjust DCG integration points
- dcg_status.go: add expanded status reporting
- dcg.go: add new DCG tooling utilities (+162 lines)

Also add scanner dashboard page and sync beads.

 {"id":"bd-21tqw","title":"Feature: Robot Restart Pane Command (--robot-restart-pane)","description":"## Overview

Add `ntm --robot-restart-pane <session> <pane> [--bead=ID]` command that provides
atomic, reliable pane restart with all edge cases handled.

## Background

Restarting a stuck or rate-limited agent pane is currently a complex manual procedure:
1. Send Ctrl-C with precise 0.1s timing gap (too slow = won't exit)
2. Wait for shell to return
3. If soft exit fails, get child PID via pgrep and kill -9
4. Wait for shell prompt
5. Launch cc
6. Wait for initialization (6s)
7. Optionally send bead assignment prompt

This is error-prone and timing-sensitive. One wrong step and you have to start over.

## Proposed Interface

```bash
ntm --robot-restart-pane myproject 4 --bead=bd-3kmf
```

## Proposed Output

```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "pane": 4,
  "success": true,
  "exit_method": "soft",       // "soft" (Ctrl-C worked) or "kill" (needed kill -9)
  "old_pid": 12345,
  "new_pid": 54321,
  "agent_type": "claude",
  "bead_assigned": "bd-3kmf",  // null if --bead not provided
  "prompt_sent": true,
  "timing": {
    "exit_ms": 150,
    "restart_ms": 6200,
    "total_ms": 6350
  }
}
```

## Algorithm

```
1. Get current child PID (for verification)
2. Attempt soft exit:
   a. Send Ctrl-C
   b. Sleep 100ms
   c. Send Ctrl-C
   d. Wait up to 3s for process to exit
3. If soft exit failed:
   a. Get child PID via pgrep -P
   b. kill -9 <child_pid>
   c. Wait for shell prompt (up to 3s)
4. Verify shell returned (check pane output for prompt)
5. Send "cc" Enter
6. Wait for cc initialization (check for ready indicator)
7. If --bead provided:
   a. Construct bead assignment prompt
   b. Send prompt
8. Return structured result
```

## Flags

- `--bead=<ID>`: Assign this bead after restart (uses standard marching orders template)
- `--prompt=<TEXT>`: Send custom prompt instead of bead template
- `--no-verify`: Skip verification steps (faster but less safe)
- `--timeout=<MS>`: Override default timeouts

## Error Handling

- If pane doesn't exist: return error with available panes
- If exit fails after kill -9: return error with diagnostic info
- If cc fails to start: return error with pane output
- All errors include enough info to diagnose without manual inspection

## Why This Matters

This is the single most common recovery operation. Making it atomic and reliable
eliminates the #1 time sink in swarm management.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:15:39.223896546Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:22:48.760940110Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:22:48.760848938Z","close_reason":"Implemented --robot-restart-pane flag","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-21tqw","depends_on_id":"bd-258a1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

Implement the core library that provides:
- detection of sensitive strings (secrets first; optional high-confidence PII)
- deterministic redaction
- structured results for callers (CLI/robot/UI)

## Package / Location
- Implement as `internal/redaction` (shared, non-CLI).
- Goal: this becomes the single source of truth used by:
  - checkpoint export (`internal/checkpoint/export.go`) via `--redact-secrets`
  - `ntm send`, `ntm copy`, `ntm save`
  - Agent Mail payloads
  - REST/WS layers + notifications + support bundles

## API Sketch
- `type Mode string` (`off|warn|redact|block`)
- `type Finding struct { Category, Match, Redacted, Start, End }`
- `type Result struct { Mode Mode; Findings []Finding; Output string; Blocked bool }`
- `func ScanAndRedact(input string, cfg Config) Result`

## Implementation Notes
- Prefer small, explicit regex set + fast-path checks.
- Avoid catastrophic regex patterns; include benchmarks if needed.
- Redaction placeholder hashing:
  - `sha256(category + ":" + match)` → hex → truncate 8
  - placeholder must be fixed-length (do not leak secret length)
- Provide helpers for both `string` and `[]byte` inputs to minimize allocations.

- Library compiles on Go 1.25+.
- No external dependencies unless absolutely necessary.
- Includes category-aware placeholders.

 {"id":"bd-2f6wa","title":"Task: Implement handoff generator from output","description":"## Overview
Implement the handoff generator that creates handoff content by analyzing agent output, transcripts, and git state.

## Background & Reasoning
Auto-generated handoffs must capture:
1. What was accomplished (from output analysis + git diff)
2. Current state (files modified, tests run)
3. What to do next (from agent's last statements)
4. Decisions and findings (inferred from conversation)

This powers the pre-compact hook for automatic context preservation.

## Deliverables

### 1. Create internal/handoff/generator.go
```go
package handoff

import (
    "bufio"
    "bytes"
    "encoding/json"
    "fmt"
    "log/slog"
    "os"
    "os/exec"
    "path/filepath"
    "regexp"
    "strings"
    "time"
)

// Generator creates handoff content from various sources
type Generator struct {
    projectDir string
    logger     *slog.Logger
}

// NewGenerator creates a Generator for the given project
func NewGenerator(projectDir string) *Generator {
    return &Generator{
        projectDir: projectDir,
        logger:     slog.Default().With("component", "handoff.generator"),
    }
}

// GenerateFromOutput creates a handoff by analyzing agent output text
func (g *Generator) GenerateFromOutput(sessionName string, output []byte) (*Handoff, error) {
    g.logger.Debug("generating handoff from output",
        "session", sessionName,
        "output_size", len(output),
    )

    h := &Handoff{
        Session: sessionName,
        Date:    time.Now().Format("2006-01-02"),
    }

    analysis := g.analyzeOutput(output)

    // Map analysis to handoff fields
    h.Goal = analysis.accomplishment
    h.Now = analysis.nextStep
    h.DoneThisSession = analysis.tasks
    h.Blockers = analysis.blockers
    h.Decisions = analysis.decisions
    h.Next = analysis.todos

    // Infer status
    if len(analysis.blockers) > 0 {
        h.Status = StatusBlocked
        h.Outcome = OutcomePartialMinus
    } else if analysis.accomplishment != "" {
        h.Status = StatusComplete
        h.Outcome = OutcomeSucceeded
    } else {
        h.Status = StatusPartial
        h.Outcome = OutcomePartialPlus
    }

    // Enrich with git state
    if err := g.EnrichWithGitState(h); err != nil {
        g.logger.Warn("git enrichment failed", "error", err)
        // Non-fatal
    }

    g.logger.Info("generated handoff from output",
        "session", sessionName,
        "goal_len", len(h.Goal),
        "now_len", len(h.Now),
        "task_count", len(h.DoneThisSession),
        "blocker_count", len(h.Blockers),
    )

    return h, nil
}

// GenerateFromTranscript creates handoff from Claude Code transcript
// Transcript path: ~/.claude/projects/.../session.jsonl
func (g *Generator) GenerateFromTranscript(sessionName, transcriptPath string) (*Handoff, error) {
    g.logger.Debug("generating handoff from transcript",
        "session", sessionName,
        "path", transcriptPath,
    )

    h := &Handoff{
        Session: sessionName,
        Date:    time.Now().Format("2006-01-02"),
    }

    file, err := os.Open(transcriptPath)
    if err != nil {
        g.logger.Error("failed to open transcript",
            "path", transcriptPath,
            "error", err,
        )
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("open transcript: %w", err)
    }
    defer file.Close()

    var (
        toolCalls      []string
        lastAssistant  string
        errors         []string
        filesModified  []string
    )

    scanner := bufio.NewScanner(file)
    scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 1024*1024), 10*1024*1024) // Handle large lines

    for scanner.Scan() {
        var entry map[string]interface{}
        if err := json.Unmarshal(scanner.Bytes(), &entry); err != nil {
            continue
        }

        // Extract tool calls
        if tools, ok := entry["tool_calls"].([]interface{}); ok {
            for _, t := range tools {
                if tm, ok := t.(map[string]interface{}); ok {
                    if name, ok := tm["name"].(string); ok {
                        toolCalls = append(toolCalls, name)
                    }
                    // Track file modifications
                    if name == "Edit" || name == "Write" {
                        if args, ok := tm["arguments"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
                            if path, ok := args["file_path"].(string); ok {
                                filesModified = append(filesModified, path)
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        // Extract assistant messages
        if role, _ := entry["role"].(string); role == "assistant" {
            if content, ok := entry["content"].(string); ok {
                lastAssistant = content
            }
        }

        // Extract errors
        if err, ok := entry["error"].(string); ok {
            errors = append(errors, err)
        }
    }

    // Analyze last assistant message for goal/now
    if lastAssistant != "" {
        analysis := g.analyzeOutput([]byte(lastAssistant))
        h.Goal = analysis.accomplishment
        h.Now = analysis.nextStep
        h.Next = analysis.todos
    }

    // Track files from tool calls
    h.Files.Modified = uniqueStrings(filesModified)

    // Track blockers from errors
    if len(errors) > 0 {
        h.Blockers = errors[:min(3, len(errors))] // Keep top 3 errors
        h.Status = StatusBlocked
    }

    // Log tool usage summary
    toolSummary := summarizeToolCalls(toolCalls)

    g.logger.Info("generated handoff from transcript",
        "session", sessionName,
        "tool_calls", len(toolCalls),
        "tool_summary", toolSummary,
        "files_modified", len(filesModified),
        "errors", len(errors),
    )

    // Enrich with git state
    if err := g.EnrichWithGitState(h); err != nil {
        g.logger.Warn("git enrichment failed", "error", err)
    }

    return h, scanner.Err()
}

// EnrichWithGitState adds git information to handoff
func (g *Generator) EnrichWithGitState(h *Handoff) error {
    g.logger.Debug("enriching handoff with git state")

    // Get modified files from git diff
    modified, err := g.getGitModified()
    if err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("git modified: %w", err)
    }
    // Merge with existing, don't overwrite
    h.Files.Modified = uniqueStrings(append(h.Files.Modified, modified...))

    // Get new files from git status
    created, err := g.getGitUntracked()
    if err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("git untracked: %w", err)
    }
    h.Files.Created = uniqueStrings(append(h.Files.Created, created...))

    // Get current branch for context
    branch, _ := g.getGitBranch()
    if branch != "" {
        if h.Findings == nil {
            h.Findings = make(map[string]string)
        }
        h.Findings["git_branch"] = branch
    }

    // Get recent commits (since session could have made commits)
    commits, _ := g.getRecentCommits(5)
    if len(commits) > 0 {
        h.Findings["recent_commits"] = strings.Join(commits, "; ")
    }

    g.logger.Debug("enriched with git state",
        "modified", len(h.Files.Modified),
        "created", len(h.Files.Created),
        "branch", branch,
    )

    return nil
}

// analysisResult holds extracted information from output
type analysisResult struct {
    accomplishment string
    nextStep       string
    tasks          []TaskRecord
    blockers       []string
    decisions      map[string]string
    todos          []string
}

// analyzeOutput extracts key information from agent output text
func (g *Generator) analyzeOutput(output []byte) analysisResult {
    result := analysisResult{
        decisions: make(map[string]string),
    }

    text := string(output)

    // Agent-specific accomplishment patterns
    accomplishmentPatterns := []*regexp.Regexp{
        // Claude patterns
        regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)I've completed?\s+(.+?)\.`),
        regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)Done:?\s*(.+)`),
        regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)Finished:?\s*(.+)`),
        regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)✓\s*(.+)`),
        regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)Successfully\s+(.+?)\.`),
        regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)Implemented\s+(.+?)\.`),
        // Codex patterns
        regexp.MustCompile(`\[DONE\]\s*(.+)`),
        regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)Completed task:?\s*(.+)`),
        // Gemini patterns
        regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)Task complete:?\s*(.+)`),
    }

    for _, pat := range accomplishmentPatterns {
        if match := pat.FindStringSubmatch(text); match != nil {
            result.accomplishment = strings.TrimSpace(match[1])
            break
        }
    }

    // Next step patterns
    nextPatterns := []*regexp.Regexp{
        regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)Next:?\s*(.+)`),
        regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)TODO:?\s*(.+)`),
        regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)Should do next:?\s*(.+)`),
        regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)Remaining:?\s*(.+)`),
        regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)Now (?:you should|we should|I should):?\s*(.+)`),
    }

    for _, pat := range nextPatterns {
        if match := pat.FindStringSubmatch(text); match != nil {
            result.nextStep = strings.TrimSpace(match[1])
            break
        }
    }

    // Blocker patterns
    blockerPatterns := []*regexp.Regexp{
        regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)Error:?\s*(.+)`),
        regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)Failed:?\s*(.+)`),
        regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)Blocked by:?\s*(.+)`),
        regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)Cannot proceed:?\s*(.+)`),
        regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)Unable to:?\s*(.+)`),
    }

    for _, pat := range blockerPatterns {
        matches := pat.FindAllStringSubmatch(text, 5)
        for _, m := range matches {
            result.blockers = append(result.blockers, strings.TrimSpace(m[1]))
        }
    }

    // Decision patterns
    decisionPatterns := []*regexp.Regexp{
        regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)I decided to\s+(.+?)\s+because\s+(.+?)\.`),
        regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)Chose\s+(.+?)\s+over\s+(.+?)\s+because`),
        regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)Using\s+(.+?)\s+for\s+(.+)`),
    }

    for _, pat := range decisionPatterns {
        matches := pat.FindAllStringSubmatch(text, 5)
        for _, m := range matches {
            if len(m) >= 3 {
                key := truncate(m[1], 30)
                result.decisions[key] = truncate(m[2], 50)
            }
        }
    }

    g.logger.Debug("analyzed output",
        "has_accomplishment", result.accomplishment != "",
        "has_next", result.nextStep != "",
        "blocker_count", len(result.blockers),
        "decision_count", len(result.decisions),
    )

    return result
}

// Git helper functions
func (g *Generator) getGitModified() ([]string, error) {
    cmd := exec.Command("git", "diff", "--name-only", "HEAD")
    cmd.Dir = g.projectDir
    out, err := cmd.Output()
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    return parseLines(out), nil
}

func (g *Generator) getGitUntracked() ([]string, error) {
    cmd := exec.Command("git", "ls-files", "--others", "--exclude-standard")
    cmd.Dir = g.projectDir
    out, err := cmd.Output()
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    return parseLines(out), nil
}

func (g *Generator) getGitBranch() (string, error) {
    cmd := exec.Command("git", "branch", "--show-current")
    cmd.Dir = g.projectDir
    out, err := cmd.Output()
    if err != nil {
        return "", err
    }
    return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
}

func (g *Generator) getRecentCommits(n int) ([]string, error) {
    cmd := exec.Command("git", "log", fmt.Sprintf("-%d", n), "--oneline")
    cmd.Dir = g.projectDir
    out, err := cmd.Output()
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    return parseLines(out), nil
}

// Utility functions
func parseLines(data []byte) []string {
    var lines []string
    for _, line := range bytes.Split(data, []byte("\n")) {
        s := strings.TrimSpace(string(line))
        if s != "" {
            lines = append(lines, s)
        }
    }
    return lines
}

func uniqueStrings(s []string) []string {
    seen := make(map[string]bool)
    var result []string
    for _, v := range s {
        if !seen[v] {
            seen[v] = true
            result = append(result, v)
        }
    }
    return result
}

func truncate(s string, max int) string {
    if len(s) <= max {
        return s
    }
    return s[:max]
}

func summarizeToolCalls(calls []string) string {
    counts := make(map[string]int)
    for _, c := range calls {
        counts[c]++
    }
    var parts []string
    for tool, count := range counts {
        parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d", tool, count))
    }
    return strings.Join(parts, ",")
}

func min(a, b int) int {
    if a < b {
        return a
    }
    return b
}

    // Option 2: /proc filesystem (more portable)

}
```

```json
{
  "panes": [{
    "index": 2,
    "pid": 12345,        // Shell PID (existing)
    "child_pid": 12346   // NEW: Agent process PID
  }]
}
```

## Edge Cases

- Multiple children: Return first (most likely the agent)
- No children: Return null (agent may have exited)
- Permission denied: Return null with error flag

 {"id":"bd-3ejl","title":"Add ntm send --base-prompt flag - prepend common instructions to all prompts automatically","description":"## Overview
Add --base-prompt flag to prepend common instructions to all prompts automatically.

## Problem
Users often want to include standard instructions (coding standards, project context, safety rules) with every prompt but don't want to repeat them.

## Solution
`ntm send myproject --base-prompt "Always run tests after changes" --msg "Implement feature X"`

The actual prompt sent becomes:
```
Always run tests after changes

Implement feature X
```

## Configuration
Can also be set in ntm.yaml:
```yaml
send:
  base_prompt: |
    Follow AGENTS.md rules.
    Run ubs before committing.
    Use br to track work.
```

## Implementation
```go
func buildPrompt(basePrompt, userPrompt string) string {
    if basePrompt == "" {
        return userPrompt
    }
    return basePrompt + "\n\n" + userPrompt
}
```

## CLI Examples
```bash
# Inline base prompt
ntm send myproject --base-prompt "Run tests" --msg "Add feature"

# From file
ntm send myproject --base-prompt-file context.md --msg "Add feature"

# With batch (prepended to each)
ntm send myproject --base-prompt "Be careful" --batch prompts.txt
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Base prompt prepended to all messages
- [ ] Works with --msg and --batch
- [ ] Config file support
- [ ] File-based base prompt (--base-prompt-file)
- [ ] Empty base prompt = no-op
- [ ] Clear separator between base and user prompt

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Prompt concatenation
- Unit test: Config file loading
- E2E test: Base prompt with batch send","notes":"## Testing Requirements

- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
